items.title: Does Altruism Exist? Implications of Selective Investment Theory for Solving Social Problems
items.author: Stephanie L. Brown, R. Michael Brown, David Cavallino
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. This article provides an overview of the debate within social psychology concerning the possible existence of altruistic motivation. After presenting the social-psychological background, we describeselective investment theory, an evolutionary theory of ...</article>

items.title: The Structure-Mapping Engine: A Multidecade Interaction Between Psychology and Artificial Intelligence
items.author: Dedre Gentner, Kenneth Forbus
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. This article describes the structure-mapping engine (SME) and its relation to psychological theory and research. SME was created in 1986 as a simulation of structure-mapping theory (SMT) and is still in use, both on its own and as part of larger scale ...</article>

items.title: Empathy for and From Embodied Robots: An Interdisciplinary Review
items.author: C. Daryl Cameron, Alan R. Wagner, Martina Orlandi, Eliana Hadjiandreou, India G. Oates, Stephen Anderson
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Several years ago, the world was stunned when the cute robot HitchBOT was destroyed. Does empathy for robots—sharing experiences and feeling compassion—make sense for humans? How do people empathize with robots, and what are the ethical and practical ...</article>
